description:
# Why

`bl remaster --detach` from a master_url repo leaves `.balls/state-repo/`
on disk: a balls-owned git clone whose `balls/tasks` branch was re-rooted
to a parentless orphan (hub ancestry severed). It is gitignored, and
since bl-f440 the canonical store moved to `.balls/worktree`, so the
leftover clone is inert *for the now-standalone repo*.

It stops being inert the moment the user re-federates. `bl remaster
<newhub> --commit` (or the per-clone form) runs `state_repo::ensure`,
which gates on the leftover:

- `first_time = !.balls/state-repo/.git exists` ‚Äî the `.git` is still
  there, so `first_time = false`: ensure only re-points
  `remote.origin.url` at the new hub.
- `warm_cache = git_state::branch_exists(&dir, "balls/tasks")` ‚Äî the
  stale detach-era `balls/tasks` is still there, so `warm_cache = true`.
- The `if !warm_cache { create_tracking_branch / create_orphan_branch;
  checkout }` block is therefore skipped entirely.

Net effect: the repo re-federates onto its own old, hub-severed orphan
instead of adopting the new hub's `balls/tasks`. No error at flip time.
The divergence only surfaces later, when `bl sync`'s non-fast-forward
push to the new hub is silently rejected ‚Äî the user believes they are
federated and sharing, and they are not. This is exactly the silent
task-drift the master_url model exists to prevent (state_repo.rs module
docs, bl-dcd3).

This footgun pre-dates bl-f440 ‚Äî pre-fix, detach left `.balls/state-repo`
as the *live* store, so the clone could not be removed. bl-f440 made it
dead weight; cleaning it up is now both safe and correct.

# Scope

Make a detach not leave a clone that a later re-federation will mistake
for a warm cache. Two candidate fixes:

- Preferred: `bl remaster --detach` removes `.balls/state-repo/` once
  the warm transplant onto `.balls/worktree` has succeeded (bl-f440's
  `adopt_state_into_worktree`). Detach owns the lifecycle of the clone
  it created; after the transplant nothing references it. Localizes the
  fix to the operation that produces the orphan.
- Alternative: `state_repo::ensure` must not treat a leftover clone
  whose `origin` URL differs from the requested `url` as a warm cache ‚Äî
  re-track the new hub's `balls/tasks` when the hub changes.

`tests/federated_hygiene.rs::remaster_detach_restores_standalone_shape`
asserts `.balls/state-repo` stays in `.gitignore` with the comment 'the
clone is still on disk'. The `.gitignore`-line assertion is unaffected
by removing the directory (gitignoring an absent path is fine), but the
comment and intent need updating to match the chosen fix.

Test: federate -> detach -> re-federate to a *different* hub; a
`bl sync` afterward must push to the new hub (the repo must be tracking
the new hub's `balls/tasks`, not its own detach-era orphan).

# Origin

Found reviewing bl-f440 (warm `remaster --detach` worktree transplant),
2026-05-20. Out of bl-f440's scope ‚Äî that ball is strictly about which
state worktree `discover` resolves post-detach; this is the separate
lifecycle question of the orphaned clone left behind.
title: Re-federation after detach silently keeps stale .balls/state-repo
